description: Software Development Life Cycle. The process of planning, creating, testing, and deploying an information system.
A flexible, iterative approach to software development focused on quick delivery and adapting to change.
Unified Modeling Language. Standard visual language used to represent and design systems.
How closely related and focused the responsibilities of a single module are. (High cohesion is good).
The degree of interdependence between software modules. (Low coupling is good).
An external entity (person or system) that interacts with the system.
A 'has-a' relationship where the child can exist independently of the parent. (Hollow diamond).
A 'part-of' relationship where the child cannot exist independently of the parent. (Filled diamond).
Verification (building the product right) and Validation (building the right product).
Software Requirements Specification. A formal document that describes in detail the requirements of a system.
Commercial Off-The-Shelf. Ready-made software available for purchase and use.
Used in Activity Diagrams to split a single flow into multiple parallel flows and re-combine them.
Focusing on essential features while ignoring unnecessary details.
A study to decide whether or not the proposed system is worthwhile.
